name: Route of the Battle of Alarcos — circular from Poblete
name_original: Ruta de la Batalla de Alarcos — circular desde Poblete
region_intro: Hiking around Poblete offers a diverse landscape characterized by historical trails, volcanic features, and natural lagoons. The region provides a mix of terrain, from relatively flat paths to routes with gentle elevation gains. These hiking trails near Poblete traverse areas that showcase both natural beauty and historical significance, making for varied outdoor experiences. The network of routes caters to different preferences, including circular walks and trails leading through distinct geological formations. Best hiking trails in Poblete The most popular hiking route is Alarcos Trail, an 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route explores the historical Alarcos area with moderate elevation changes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Battle of Alarcos — circular from Poblete, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This circular route starts from Poblete and offers insights into the historical landscape of the Battle of Alarcos. Local hikers also love the Posadilla Volcano and Lagoon Loop, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through volcanic features and a lagoon, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Hiking around Poblete is defined by historical routes, volcanic landscapes, and natural lagoons. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging ability levels, ensuring a suitable trail for most hikers. The routes in Poblete are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Poblete's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Poblete?
A: There are over 100 hiking trails around Poblete, offering a diverse range of experiences for all skill levels.

Q: Are there easy hikes suitable for beginners in Poblete?
A: Yes, Poblete offers around 45 easy hiking routes. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198498641/alarcos-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.6 km long and features gentle elevation changes, making it ideal for a relaxed walk.

Q: Can I find circular walks around Poblete?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198486760/route-of-the-battle-of-alarcos-circular-from-poblete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Battle of Alarcos — circular from Poblete&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 9.2 km loop that starts directly from Poblete.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes near Poblete?
A: While most routes are easy to moderate, there is at least one challenging hike available for those seeking a more strenuous adventure. The region's terrain, characterized by volcanic features and historical paths, offers varied difficulty levels.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes around Poblete?
A: Hiking around Poblete will expose you to unique natural features such as volcanic landscapes and natural lagoons. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197977248/posadilla-volcano-and-lagoon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Posadilla Volcano and Lagoon Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, taking you through these distinct geological formations.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the area is rich in historical significance and natural beauty. You can explore sites related to the Battle of Alarcos or visit natural highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Posadilla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3554273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198513805/alarcos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Trail&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores the historical Alarcos area.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Poblete?
A: The hiking routes in Poblete are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscape, which combines historical trails with volcanic features and natural lagoons.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Poblete?
A: Yes, many of the easier routes are suitable for families. The region's trails offer a mix of terrain, with numerous paths that are relatively flat or have gentle elevation gains, perfect for outings with children.

Q: Is there public transport access to the hiking trails around Poblete?
A: While specific public transport routes directly to trailheads may vary, Poblete is generally accessible. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33707930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Detour – Start of the Greenway loop from Ciudad Real&lt;/a&gt;, you might find public transport options to nearby towns like Ciudad Real, which can serve as a starting point.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Poblete?
A: The region generally offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the trails in Poblete?
A: Yes, the varied terrain around Poblete includes several spots offering scenic views. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Posadilla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent vistas, particularly over the lagoon area.

Q: Where can I park when visiting the hiking trails in Poblete?
A: Parking availability can vary by trailhead. Many routes, especially those starting from Poblete itself or other nearby towns, will have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for parking information relevant to your chosen trail.
tips: Technical section especially if you go down, do it carefully.

The sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de Alarcos is a Gothic-style hermitage located in Alarcos, declared a Site of Cultural Interest in 1980. Gothic style and basilical in plan, a large tracery rose window opens above its door. Their pilgrimage is celebrated on Pentecost Monday.

It is part of the Alarcos-Calatrava Archaeological Park.
The castle is at the top, built on an artificial platform. Its plan is rectangular and it has nine towers, seven square and two pentagonal in the bow. It was occupied by Iberians, Romans and Arabs.
In 1195 a great battle took place here, the Battle of Alarcos against the Almohads, in which Alfonso VIII had to flee wounded in the leg. After the war, Alarcos and its surroundings remained in Almohad power, which delayed the Reconquest for several decades.
This Battle took place in the plain that today extends between Alarcos and Poblete.

The Battle of Alarcos Route is a fascinating experience that combines history and technology. This route allows visitors to explore the historic site of the battle that took place on July 19, 1195, where the Almohad troops of Al-Mansur defeated the Christian troops commanded by the Castilian king Alfonso VIII.
Route of historical value that will allow us to know both the Battle of Alarcos and the site.
